# Senior Data Scientist – Machine Learning & Analytics (Direct-to-Consumer Insights Division)
[...]
Welcome to arenaflex, where we believe in the power of data to transform how businesses connect with their customers. As a leader in the entertainment and streaming industry, arenaflex operates a vast ecosystem of content and product offerings that touch millions of lives around the world. Our Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) Information Science group is at the heart of this transformation, joining profound data investigation and analysis of our rich customer information to present compelling visions around client retention and preferences across our extensive product offerings and content.
[...]
Within arenaflex, we serve as the insights and modeling partners for the content, marketing, product, and engineering teams across our streaming platforms. As Data Scientists at arenaflex, we leverage data to empower leaders with information, predictions, and insights that ultimately impact the experiences of millions of customers worldwide. If you are someone who loves to work cross-functionally, build models, perform statistical analysis, and create visualizations to provide scalable and iterative capabilities, then this is an exceptional opportunity for you to join our team!
[...]
to join our growing Information Science team in Santa Monica. In this role, you will work alongside a team of brilliant data scientists to solve sophisticated business problems using data science and machine learning. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams—including content, product, and marketing—to understand their needs and develop solutions that meet their requirements. Additionally, you'll play a key role in interpreting your discoveries for both technical experts and those without a technical background.
[...]
This position offers an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of data science, machine learning, and business strategy, contributing to decisions that shape the future of entertainment for millions of viewers globally.
[...]
As a Senior Data Scientist at arenaflex, your responsibilities will span the full data science lifecycle, from data acquisition to insight delivery. Here's what you can expect:

highlights:
Associate 3D Artist | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# Associate 3D Artist

Posted Jun 6, 2026

Warner Bros. Discovery is a leading entertainment company that focuses on creating iconic content and beloved brands. The Associate 3D Artist role involves collaborating with the art team to create visual game components, handling art tasks, and exploring new tools and workflows including AI-assisted creative tools.

### Responsibilities

- Work with the art team to create new visual game components
- Rendering 3D deliverables using Maya/Arnold/Blender or equivalent software
- Rigging, topology cleanup, and asset optimization
- Taking assets through the entire 3D pipeline
- Integrating art assets and maintaining naming conventions
- Help create visually compelling assets for marketing and user acquisition, including support for interactive creative formats such as HTML playables
- Explore and apply AI-enabled tools and workflows
- Ability to take direction well
- Strong problem solving skills
- Ability to learn new tools quickly
- Self-motivated and eager to learn

Skills

- Experience with Maya, ZBrush, Substance, Adobe Photoshop, and other digital content creation software
- Experience with Unity and game development environments
- Familiarity with emerging AI-assisted creative tools or an interest in learning how to incorporate them into art workflows
- A portfolio demonstrating your work and skills. No art test required, your portfolio should speak for itself!
- Ability to take direction well
- Strong problem solving skills
- Ability to learn new tools quickly
- Self-motivated and eager to learn
- Interest in or exposure to interactive ad formats or HTML playables is a strong plus
- The ability to work and collaborate with a small team. Experience working on a game development team is a plus

### Benefits

- Health insurance coverage
- An employee wellness program
- Life and disability insurance
- A retirement savings plan
- Paid holidays and sick time
- Vacation

Company Overview

- Warner Bros. It was founded in 1985, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is

results.title: **Experienced Full Stack Data Scientist – AI and Factual Displaying for Streaming Business** | Hirevector
highlights:
**Experienced Full Stack Data Scientist – AI and Factual Displaying for Streaming Business** | Hirevector
[...]
# **Experienced Full Stack Data Scientist – AI and Factual Displaying for Streaming Business**
[...]
Are you a data-driven professional with a passion for AI and factual displaying? Do you want to join a dynamic team that's shaping the future of streaming entertainment? Look no further! arenaflex, a leading innovator in direct-to-consumer video services, is seeking an experienced Full Stack Data Scientist to join our Information Science team.
[...]
As a Full Stack Data Scientist at arenaflex, you'll play a critical role in driving business growth and decision-making through data-driven insights. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams to develop and implement AI and factual displaying solutions that drive user engagement, retention, and revenue growth. If you're a seasoned data professional with a passion for storytelling and a knack for communicating complex ideas, we want to hear from you!
[...]
- Support the streaming business with AI and factual displaying focused on understanding and predicting user behavior, including churn, upgrade/downgrade, cost responsiveness, customer segmentation, survival analysis, A/B testing, and causal inference.
- Develop and implement innovative AI strategies to address key business questions while identifying new areas to explore.
- Drive experimentation to assess the effects of your models and iterate on your approach.
[...]
- Collaborate closely with colleagues across the organization to identify and capitalize on opportunities.
- Present your findings and recommendations to senior leaders.
- Work with other data teams to improve platform capabilities around data modeling, data representation, experimentation, and data design.
[...]
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Science, Physics, Econometrics, or Statistics.
- 3+ years of experience designing, building, and evaluating scalable AI solutions.
- Strong coding experience in one or more data programming languages like Python/R, with additional experience in scientific libraries like NumPy, Pandas, or similar libraries.
- Proficiency in analyzing data and creating ML models using Python (with ML frameworks like TensorFlow, scikit-learn, etc.).
- Strong foundation in statistical modeling: regression, clustering, time series estimation, causal inference, and other methods.
[...]
- Experience creating interpretable numerical models and solving complex quantitative problems that can be understood by non-technical stakeholders.
[...]
- Ability to extract insights and recommendations from data to inform model development and business decisions.
[...]
- Willingness to collaborate with other data researchers, data analysts, and key stakeholders.
- Strong understanding of SQL.
[...]
**Preferred Capabilities**
[...]
- Postgraduate education in a quantitative field.
- Excellent analytical skills, with a high level of statistical knowledge.
- Demonstrated ability to choose the right statistical tools given a data analysis problem.
- Ability to adapt quickly in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities.
- Strong relational skills, for both technical and non-technical audiences.
- Ability to focus on multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines.
- Experience with data platforms and applications like Databricks, Jupyter, Snowflake, Wind current, and GitHub.
- Experience with designing and analyzing A/B testing and other experimentation types.
- Experience thinking critically to interpret market and customer data, ideally about a subscription-based service.
- Experience with distributed data technologies (Hive, Hadoop).
- Experience with NLP and NN models.
[...]
If you're a data-driven professional with a passion for AI and factual displaying, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity to join our team at arenaflex.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Studio Media Algorithms | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Studio Media Algorithms | Wfhforgeon

Wfhforgeon.← All Jobs

# Machine Learning Scientist, Studio Media Algorithms

Posted Jun 1, 2026

- Job Description:
- Design, train/post-train/fine-tune, and evaluate foundational algorithmic solutions with applications in a variety of domains in the VFX, animation, and games space
- Develop reusable foundational components and best practices, such as dataset curation, large-scale training, and post-training processes
- Work cross-functionally with engineers, scientists, artists, and product leaders to help identify and prioritize strategic research investment opportunities and problem requirements
- Expand technical depth and domain expertise into new/adjacent areas as the business needs and the state of technology evolve

- Requirements:
- Research experience with a successful track record of delivering quality results and/or academic publications in top ML, CV, CG, or NLP venues
- Deep familiarity with modern generative model architectures, including diffusion models (e.g., MMDiT) and/or autoregressive models (e.g., GPT-style)
- Experience with the full stack of model development: data curation, annotation/captioning, distributed training, post-training techniques (e.g., fine-tuning, RL), and/or robust evaluation
- Expertise in designing and training deep learning (DL) architectures for media understanding and generation
- Deep mathematical skills with knowledge of statistical methods and optimization
- Extensive experience with DL frameworks such as Tensorflow or PyTorch
- Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, Java, or C++
- Advanced degree in Computer Science or related field

- Benefits:
- Inclusion is a Netflix value
- Flexible work arrangements

results.title: Manager, Software Engineering, Ad Experiences | Jobflarely
highlights: , “the stuff dreams are made of,” we’re not just referring to the world of wizards, dragons and superheroes, or even to the wonders of Planet Earth. Behind WBD’s vast portfolio of iconic content and beloved brands, are the storytellers bringing our characters to life, the creators bringing them to your living rooms and the dreamers creating what’s next… From brilliant creatives, to technology trailblazers, across the globe, WBD offers career defining opportunities, thoughtfully curated benefits, and the tools to explore and grow into your best selves. Here you are supported, here you are celebrated, here you can thrive. We are the now and the next. The power behind the people building the future. We are born from the spirit of innovation. We are created from the idea that people around the world want more, need more, deserve more. We are the home of the global digital revolution. We are CNN. To see what it’s like to work at CNN, follow @WBDLife on Instagram and X! The CNN Engineering team is looking for an Engineering Manager to lead our Ad Experiences team. This is a unique opportunity for someone passionate about advertising technologies, user experience, and data-driven decision-making to help shape the future of ad delivery and monetization across CNN’s digital platforms. As an Engineering Manager, you’ll lead a team of 6 engineers focused on building and optimizing ad experiences that balance revenue goals with seamless user engagement. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners in product, design, and data to deliver high-impact solutions that power CNN’s advertising ecosystem. Your New Role... Lead, mentor, and grow a high-performing team of engineers focused on ad technologies and user experience. Drive the technical strategy and execution for ad integrations, including display, video, and programmatic ads. Collaborate with stakeholders to define and prioritize work that aligns with business goals and user needs. Guide the team through technical planning, discovery, and delivery of scalable ad solutions. Provide technical leadership through code reviews, architectural guidance, and hands-on contributions when needed. Ensure high standards of quality, performance, and reliability in all ad-related features and systems. Your Role Accountabilities... Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent experience. Proven experience managing and growing engineering teams (5+ years preferred). Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate across technical and non-technical teams. Experience hiring, onboarding, and mentoring engineers. Demonstrated success in delivering performant, scalable consumer-facing applications. Platform Experience Full-stack development experience with JavaScript, modern frameworks, HTML/CSS, and responsive design; Python experience is a plus. Experience with AWS or other cloud platforms for scalable infrastructure. Familiarity with A/B testing and consumer testing platforms to optimize ad performance and user experience. Knowledge of operational tools such as PagerDuty and experience managing an on-call team. Ad Tech Experience- Deep understanding of ad development and integration on publisher websites for display and video ad formats, using Supply-Side Platforms (SSPs), such as Google Ad Manager and Freewheel, and other 3rd party ad technology, business development and affiliate marketing solutions. Nice to Haves... Proficiency with ad testing tools and industry best practices for validation and optimization. Strong grasp of ad performance metrics, real-time monitoring, and analytics interpretation. Experience developing scalable solutions for high-traffic, content-rich websites. Quick adaptability to emerging technologies, frameworks, and evolving ad tech ecosystems. A user-first mindset with a passion for balancing monetization strategies and seamless user experience. You’re passionate about building great consumer experiences to audiences at scale.

results.title: Software Engineer I | Hirequorum
highlights:
Software Engineer I | Hirequorum

Hirequorum.← All Jobs

# Software Engineer I

Posted Jun 7, 2026

Warner Bros. Discovery is a leading media and entertainment company known for its iconic content and storytelling. They are seeking a Software Engineer I to join the Interactive News team, where the role involves building and maintaining interactive features for CNN digital storytelling while collaborating with various teams to enhance user experiences.

### Responsibilities

- Build and maintain interactive features and editorial tools used across CNN.com
- Develop modern web applications using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS with a focus on accessibility, performance, and responsive design
- Collaborate with engineers, designers, and newsroom partners to implement new features
- Contribute to code reviews and help improve the quality of the codebase
- Participate in agile development processes including planning, demos, and retrospectives
- Learn from senior engineers through mentorship and collaboration
- Help support systems during major news events and high traffic moments

Skills

- Experience building web applications using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS
- Understanding of responsive design and semantic HTML
- Familiarity with Git and modern development workflows
- Interest in learning full stack development and cloud based systems
- Experience or exposure to testing frameworks such as Jest, Mocha, or Jasmine
- Strong problem solving skills and curiosity to learn new technologies
- Strong collaboration and communication skills
- Interest in journalism, storytelling, or mission driven technology
- Experience working with content management systems used in news or publishing
- Familiarity with Python or other scripting languages
- Experience building data visualizations or interactive storytelling tools
- Exposure to cloud infrastructure or DevOps tooling
- Experience supporting applications in production environments

### Benefits

- Health insurance coverage
- An employee wellness program
- Life and disability insurance
- A retirement savings plan
- Paid holidays
- Sick time
- Vacation

Company Overview

- Warner Bros. It was founded in 1985, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is https://wbd.com. Company H1B Sponsorship

- Warner Bros. Discovery has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 5 in 2026, 31 in 2025, 34 in 2024, 47 in 2023, 72 in 2022, 82 in 2021, 30 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: Analyst, CRM | Wfh
highlights: technology trailblazers, across the globe, WBD offers career defining opportunities, thoughtfully curated benefits, and the tools to explore and grow into your best selves. Here you are supported, here you are celebrated, here you can thrive. WB Games is a leader in the games industry across all game platforms and mobile devices. Our diverse and inclusive workforce creates and brings to life the most beloved characters, franchises, heroes, and wizards in the world. We are fans of what we make and proud of what we do. We have studios and offices across the globe. The Job WB Games is recruiting a CRM Analyst under the Global Sales & Marketing organization. This role will take part in strengthening WB Game’s email and lifecycle marketing programs by crafting targeted and insights-driven campaigns and player journeys with a goal of retaining, engaging, and monetizing fans of our gamer audience and fans of the franchise. The Ideal candidate can adapt between marketing strategy, campaign management, as well as operational and analytical needs. They are a core or casual gamer with familiarity and experience with player-facing content (web, email, in-game promotion, social/influencer, community & streaming, etc.), an understanding how to scale personalized messaging per game from nascency to best-in-class, and a keen interest in leveraging analysis and data integration to enable the most cutting-edge marketing and direct-digital messaging capabilities possible. The Daily Execute and Manage CRM Campaigns: Manage (end-to-end) on behalf of our game franchises - including the building and maintaining of marketing channel automations and template frameworks that support dynamic player journeys Plan and Coordinate A/B Tests: Design and launch multi-variant campaigns experiments that optimize email content, timing, and segmentation Aligned Execution: Work closely with colleagues in analytics, brand marketing, product marketing, and creative services to ensure alignment in execution on test measurement and audience messaging Dashboard & Reporting: Build and maintain dashboards and reports for internal reviews on performance at all dimensions for specific marketing channel and game combinations, exposing the trends and opportunities based on data driven insights Support CRM & Department leads with presentations, evaluations and implementation strategies that support the growth of the CRM program The Essentials Bachelor's Degree and 2 - 5 years of CRM or digital marketing experience Experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze, CleverTap, Adobe Experience, Cloud or similar lifecycle marketing platforms for email, in-app messaging, push notifications, etc. Experience with data visualization tools like Looker or Tableau Proficient with Adobe Photoshop Basic HTML skills AMPscript Familiarity in gaming and entertainment industry How We Get Things Done… This last bit is probably the most important! Here at WBD, our guiding principles are the core values by which we operate and are central to how we get things done. You can find them at along with some insights from the team on what they mean and how they show up in their day to day. We hope they resonate with you and look forward to discussing them during your interview. Championing Inclusion at WBD Warner Bros. Discovery embraces the opportunity to build a workforce that reflects a wide array of perspectives, backgrounds and experiences. Being an equal opportunity employer means that we take seriously our responsibility to consider qualified candidates on the basis of merit, without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, mental or physical disability, and genetic information, marital status, citizenship status, military status, protected veteran status or any other category protected by law. If you’re a qualified candidate with a disability and you require adjustments or accommodations during the job application

results.title: How Warner Bros. Discovery Is Using AI for Advertising Sales
highlights:
# How Warner Bros. Discovery Is Using AI for Advertising Sales

Published: 2026-06-09T19:00:00+00:00
Source: deloitte.wsj.com (deloitte.wsj.com)
Language: en

## Story

Bridget Jayaram, vice president of data-driven ad sales at Warner Bros. Discovery, offers this advice for advertisers: “Don’t be afraid of AI. Lean in and learn.”

“AI will make day-to-day operations much more efficient so that we can all focus on doing what humans do well—building relationships and developing creative programs that move the needle,” Jayaram explains in a recent video series on the future of advertising.

At Warner Bros. Discovery, Jayaram and her team are testing multiple applications of AI. This includes modernizing the tech stack to streamline the process of ad buying across platforms. Previously, she says, advertisers might have to use five or more systems to make a cross-channel ad buy.

“As you can imagine, a legacy TV business has a lot of systems that don’t work in the same way as digital,” she says. “Now, we’re bringing those systems together into one interface and layering on AI agents to help our team be more efficient and effective.”

Warner Bros. Discovery is also exploring ways to use AI to help establish contextual alignment in channels where advertisers have been traditionally wary of brand safety, including live TV news (Warner Bros. Discovery owns CNN). “There’s a lot of lifestyle, travel, and cooking content within our live TV portfolio—and right now, this is a big blind spot for many advertisers.”

Equally important in targeting ads is a sound data strategy, particularly because modern consumers could be viewing content on many different devices, Jayaram notes. To that end, Warner Bros. Discovery is connecting data from linear TV, cellphones, and other devices to better measure whole-home viewership.

“There’s a lot of noise in the marketplace, and we want consumers to be excited about what they’re seeing,” she says. “We don’t want to run into creative fatigue or diminishing returns because we’re bombarding users with ad after ad after ad.”

This article is part of an ongoing series of interviews with executives. The executives’ participation in these articles are solely for educational purposes based on their knowledge of the subject and the views expressed by them are solely their own. This article should not be deemed or construed to be for the purpose of soliciting business for any of the companies mentioned, nor does Deloitte advocate or endorse the services or products provided by these companies. This article contains general information only and Deloitte is not, by means of this article, rendering accounting, business, financial, investment, legal, tax, or other professional advice or services. This article is not a substitute for such professional advice or services, nor should it be used as a basis for any decision or action that may affect your business. Before making any decision or taking any action that may affect your business, you should consult a qualified professional advisor. Deloitte shall not be responsible for any loss sustained by any person who relies on this article. Deloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (“DTTL”), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as “Deloitte Global”) does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the “Deloitte” name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Please see www.deloitte.com/about to learn more about our global network of member firms.

Your New Role We are seeking a results-oriented Senior Director, eDiscovery & Forensics to lead the enterprise-wide strategy and execution of legal discovery and forensic investigation capabilities. This role ensures defensible, scalable processes for litigation support, regulatory compliance, and cyber incident response across the company. The Senior Director oversees key programs including electronic discovery (ESI preservation, collection, processing, and review), forensic investigations for cyber incidents and insider threats, and governance for evidence handling and chain-of-custody protocols. The role also drives compliance alignment with global privacy and data protection regulations and partners closely with Legal, Compliance, Privacy, Cybersecurity, and Technology teams to deliver secure, efficient, and defensible outcomes. The successful candidate demonstrates sound judgment, strong execution discipline, clear executive communication skills, and experience leading globally distributed teams in complex, matrixed environments. Your Role Accountabilities Own the enterprise eDiscovery and Forensics strategy, ensuring defensible, efficient processes for litigation, regulatory, and internal investigations. Establish governance and standards for evidence handling, chain of custody, and compliance with global privacy and data protection laws (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA). Lead forensic readiness and investigation programs, enabling rapid triage, root-cause analysis, and defensible reporting for cyber incidents, insider threats, and fraud cases. Oversee eDiscovery workflows, including preservation, collection, processing, and review of electronically stored information (ESI) for litigation and regulatory matters. Manage platform governance for eDiscovery and forensic tools (e.g., Relativity, Nuix, EnCase, FTK, Cellebrite), ensuring security, access controls, and operational efficiency. Drive automation and workflow optimization, including legal hold notifications, custodian tracking, and review processes to improve efficiency. Partner with Legal, Compliance, Privacy, and Cybersecurity teams to align discovery and investigation processes with enterprise standards and regulatory requirements. Ensure compliance alignment with regulatory and industry frameworks (e.g., SOX IT controls, PCI DSS, SWIFT, privacy and data protection requirements, or similar standards) and contractual obligations; support audits and client security reviews. Provide executive-level reporting on case metrics, investigation turnaround times, and cost efficiency to senior leadership and governance bodies. Build and scale a high-performing global team, fostering technical excellence, accountability, and a culture of partnership across legal and technology functions. Serve as a trusted advisor, providing expert guidance and defensible testimony when required in legal or regulatory proceedings. Qualifications & Experience To succeed in this role, you will need: A minimum of 12+ years in digital forensics, eDiscovery, cybersecurity investigations, incident response, or related technical roles. Proven senior leadership in eDiscovery and digital forensics, with at least 5 years in leadership roles managing large-scale litigation support and cyber investigations. Deep expertise in forensic tools and techniques (EnCase, FTK, Cellebrite) and eDiscovery platforms (Relativity, Nuix), with strong understanding of EDR and cloud forensics. Proven ability to manage complex investigations and discovery processes in compliance with global privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA) and data governance frameworks. Strong
